symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple rough, exophytic, dome-shaped hyperkeratotic papules on the dorsal hands and fingers, spread from a single lesion over 3 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rough, hyperkeratotic, exophytic papules multiplying on the hands over months is the hallmark of common viral warts spreading by autoinoculation.
Molluscum lesions are smooth, waxy and umbilicated, not rough and hyperkeratotic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pinpoint black dots within the lesions and disrupted skin (dermatoglyphic) lines, patient-visible

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pinpoint black dots (thrombosed capillaries) and interruption of normal skin lines are characteristic of verruca vulgaris; the black dots are visible to the patient and near-pathognomonic even before paring.
Thrombosed capillaries and preserved benign morphology point away from malignancy, which more often shows a solitary friable, ulcerated or irregularly pigmented lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pared lesion surface reveals pinpoint thrombosed capillaries and loss of dermatoglyphics; no ulceration, no friability, no irregular pigmentation; no regional lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paring that reveals pinpoint thrombosed capillaries is the classic in-person confirmatory sign of a wart. Clinician-performed — sealed; the AI Doctor approximates it from the patient-visible black dots and morphology, and cannot pare or palpate nodes remotely.
Absence of ulceration, friability, irregular pigmentation and lymphadenopathy on examination excludes malignancy at this encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of picking, biting and tweezing the lesions with temporally-associated spread (autoinoculation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical trauma from picking/biting releases HPV into micro-abrasions on adjacent skin, explaining the rapid periungual and multi-digit spread — a mechanism specific to warts/molluscum rather than malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Periungual lesions painful on pressure, limiting fine-motor tasks (typing, writing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Periungual and pressure-point warts are commonly painful; supports the diagnosis weakly and explains the functional impact, but pain is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spontaneous bleeding, no dark/irregular pigmentation, no ulceration, no solitary rapidly-enlarging lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding, colour change, ulceration and a dominant rapidly-growing lesion makes cutaneous malignancy very unlikely and keeps it as a safety-net trigger rather than a present concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: No surrounding erythema, warmth, swelling, pus, ascending red streaks, or fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of local infection signs and ascending streaks makes secondary bacterial infection from picking unlikely at this encounter, keeping it as a safety-net trigger.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for clinically characteristic multiple verrucae. Reserve for a solitary lesion with atypical or malignant features (bleeding, ulceration, irregular pigmentation, rapid growth) or for treatment-refractory single lesions — the AI Doctor should not arrange urgent biopsy of classic warts.

Useful in person to characterise a morphologically atypical lesion and distinguish a wart from SCC/amelanotic melanoma; not obtainable via chat and not needed for the classic multiple-wart picture here.
